title: Spaghetti Bolognese
description: The weeknight classic. Slow-simmered mince, rich tinned tomato, a knob of butter to finish.

steps.detail: Halve and peel the onion, keeping the root on to stop your eyes watering. Make lengthwise cuts, then across — aim for small, even dice (about 5mm). Smash garlic with the flat of a knife to loosen the skin, then mince or grate. Having everything prepped before you start the heat is called mise en place — makes the cook relaxed, not frantic.

steps.content: Brown mince in olive oil, 6 min.
steps.detail: Heat a glug of oil in a heavy pan over medium-high until it shimmers. Crumble the mince in in large chunks — don't break it up straight away. Let it sit untouched for 2 min to deep-brown on the bottom. Then start breaking up with a wooden spoon as you stir. That browning is flavour. Cook until no pink remains.

steps.content: Add onion and garlic, cook until soft.
steps.detail: Drop heat to medium. Tip in onion and garlic — they'll sizzle and soften in the mince fat. Cook 5 min, stirring occasionally, until the onion is translucent and starting to gold at the edges. If the pan looks dry, a splash of water helps. Season with a big pinch of salt now.

Crush whole tomatoes with your hand as you pour them in. Add a pinch of salt, a crack of pepper, and a knob of butter if you've got one — it rounds off the acid. Simmer uncovered on low — big lazy bubbles, not a rolling boil — for 20 min, stirring every few minutes. It should reduce to a rich, glossy sauce that coats a spoon.

Bring a big pot of water to a rolling boil. Salt it heavily — it should taste like sea water. Add spaghetti, stir to prevent clumping. Cook 1 min less than the packet time for al dente. Before draining, scoop out a cup of pasta water — the starchy water emulsifies the sauce and pasta.

steps.content: Toss through sauce, top with parmesan.
steps.detail: Drag the pasta straight from the pot into the sauce with tongs — a little water clinging is fine. Toss for a minute over low heat. If the sauce looks tight, splash in pasta water. Parmesan grated over the top, crack of pepper, eat immediately while it's still steaming.

title: Green Thai Curry
description: Bright, punchy green curry with chicken thigh and coconut milk.

steps.content: Fry curry paste in a splash of oil, 1 min.
steps.detail: Heat a tablespoon of oil in a wide pan over medium. Spoon in the curry paste and stir constantly for a full minute — you'll see it go from matte to shiny, smell it bloom. This step wakes the paste up; skip it and the curry stays flat. If it starts to catch, drop the heat.

steps.content: Add diced chicken, toss to coat.
steps.detail: Cut thigh into bite-sized pieces (about 3cm — bigger than you think, they shrink). Tip into the paste and stir until every piece wears a green coat. Cook 2 min to just start colouring the edges — not fully cooking through yet.

steps.content: Pour in coconut milk, simmer 12 min.
steps.detail: Shake the tin first — the cream and water often separate. Pour it all in, including any solid cream on top. Stir to dissolve the paste into the coconut milk. Bring to a bare simmer (small bubbles) and cook 10–12 min until the chicken is cooked and the sauce has tightened slightly. Don't boil hard — it splits coconut milk.

steps.content: Season with fish sauce and lime.
steps.detail: Off heat for tasting (flavours are less bold hot). Stir in fish sauce, then squeeze half the lime. Taste. It should be balanced — salty from fish sauce, sour from lime, a touch sweet from the coconut. Adjust in tiny increments. More lime if flat, more fish sauce if too sweet.

Pile over jasmine rice. Tear basil (don't chop — basil bruises, turns dark). Sliced fresh chilli on top for heat. Extra lime wedges on the side. A spoon is better than a fork — you want broth with every bite.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Yoghurt-marinated chicken in a tomato-cream sauce. Better than takeaway.

steps.content: Marinate chicken in yoghurt and curry powder.
steps.detail: Cube chicken thighs into 3cm pieces. In a bowl, mix with yoghurt, curry powder, a big pinch of salt, and a squeeze of lemon if you've got one. Yoghurt tenderises the meat — its lactic acid breaks down fibres gently. 30 min is good; overnight is transformative. Cover and fridge.

steps.content: Sear chicken in hot pan, set aside.
steps.detail: Heat a splash of oil in a heavy pan until shimmering. Shake excess marinade off each piece and sear in batches — overcrowding steams. 2 min a side for deep gold char patches. Don't cook through yet; it finishes in the sauce. Remove to a plate.
Same pan — don't wash, those fond bits are flavour. Lower to medium. Finely chopped onion, a splash of oil if dry. 6 min till soft and golden at the edges. Add grated garlic the last minute.

steps.content: Add tomatoes, simmer 10 min, then cream.
steps.detail: Tip in tomatoes (crush with your hand). Stir, season, simmer 10 min uncovered till thick and the raw tomato edge is gone. Off heat, stir in cream — don't let it boil hard once cream's in, it can split.
Chicken back with any resting juices. Simmer gently 8 min till cooked through (juices run clear when pierced). Taste — salt, a pinch of sugar if too sharp.
Serve over fluffy basmati rice. Tear coriander over the top — a lot of it. A dollop of extra yoghurt on the side is lovely. Warm naan if you have it.

title: Haloumi Grain Bowl
description: Crispy haloumi, roast pumpkin, rocket, lemon. Lunch goals.
steps: Roast pumpkin with oil and cumin, 25 min at 200°C.
Cook rice.
Fry haloumi slices till golden both sides.
Assemble bowl: rice, rocket, pumpkin, haloumi.
Squeeze lemon over the lot.

title: Sausage Pasta Bake
description: Snags out of their skins, tomato, pasta, a crust of cheese. Feeds a crowd.
steps: Squeeze snag meat out of skins, brown in pan.
Add diced onion and garlic, soften.
Pour in tomatoes, simmer.
Cook pasta, reserving some water.
Combine, tip into dish, top with mozzarella.
Bake 15 min at 200°C until bubbly.

title: Sunday Roast Chook
description: Whole chicken, crispy skin, lemon and garlic.

steps.detail: Get the oven going first thing — by the time you've prepped, it's ready. A roast needs high heat. Pull the chicken out of the fridge when you turn the oven on — cold meat cooks unevenly.

steps.content: Pat chook dry, rub with oil and salt.
steps.detail: Paper towels inside and out — dry skin = crispy skin. Rub all over with olive oil, then coat heavily with sea salt and cracked pepper. Don't skimp on seasoning — a 1.6kg bird can take a good tablespoon of salt spread around. Massage into the legs, under the wings, everywhere.

steps.content: Stuff cavity with lemon and garlic.
steps.detail: Halve a lemon and a whole garlic head across the equator. Stuff inside the cavity — they steam the bird from inside and flavour the juices. A few thyme sprigs if you have them. Tying the legs with string keeps the thighs juicy but it's optional.

Chunk potatoes (skin on), toss in oil and salt. Spread in a roasting tray, sit the chicken on top breast-side-up. The chicken fat drips onto the potatoes — best roast potatoes in the world. 75 min for a 1.6kg bird: juices run clear when pierced in the thigh, and a thermometer reads 75°C in the thickest part. Baste halfway with pan juices if you remember.

Non-negotiable. Lift the chicken onto a board, tent loosely with foil. The juices that would run out onto the board now redistribute into the meat. Use the 10 min to stir pan juices into a quick gravy if you like — a splash of stock, boil hard, done.

title: Veggie Fried Rice
description: Leftover rice, a fridge raid, one pan. Dinner in 10.
steps: Scramble eggs, set aside.
Fry garlic and diced carrot.
Add peas and cold rice, toss over high heat.
Pour soy sauce down the side of the pan.
Fold in eggs and spring onion.

title: Pumpkin & Chickpea Curry
description: Sweet pumpkin, creamy coconut, warming curry powder.
steps: Soften onion with curry powder.
Add diced pumpkin, toss to coat.
Pour in coconut milk, simmer 20 min.
Add chickpeas and spinach for last 3 min.
Serve over basmati.

title: Lemon Chicken & Rice
description: One-pan, lemon-bright, weeknight winner.
steps: Sear chicken in oil, skin-side down.
Remove, add garlic and rice, toast 1 min.
Pour in stock and lemon juice.
Return chicken, cover, simmer 22 min.
Rest 5 min, scatter parsley.

title: BBQ Snag Sanga
description: Bread, snag, grilled onions, sauce. A national treasure.
steps: Fire up the BBQ.
Slice onions, fry in butter until golden.
Grill snags, turning often.
Butter bread, fold around snag and onions.

title: Prawn Pad Thai
description: Rice noodles, prawns, peanuts, lime. Takeaway at home.
steps: Mix fish sauce, peanut butter, lime for sauce.
Fry prawns 2 min, remove.
Scramble eggs in pan.
Toss cooked rice noodles with sauce (use spaghetti in a pinch).
Return prawns, top with spring onion and chilli.

title: Smashed Avo on Toast
description: The brunch standard. Sourdough, avo, feta, lemon, chilli flakes.

steps.detail: Thick-cut sourdough if you have it. Toast well — you want a sturdy base that holds avo without going soggy. Under the grill toasts more evenly than a pop-up. One side, flip, watch for deep gold.

steps.content: Mash avo with lemon and salt.
steps.detail: Ripe avo gives easily when you press near the stem. Halve, twist apart, lift out the stone. Scoop flesh into a bowl. Squeeze lemon and season with salt flakes — add lemon BEFORE mashing so it doesn't brown. Mash with a fork — chunky, not puréed. Texture is good.
Spread avo generously, right to the edges. Feta crumbled between your fingers (more rustic than cubed). Don't hold back on the feta — it balances the richness.
Good olive oil in a proper drizzle. Chilli flakes or fresh chilli if you've got it. A final crack of salt on top. Eat immediately — avo toast waits for no one.

title: Beef Tacos
description: Soft tortillas, spiced mince, avo, lime.

steps.content: Brown mince with cumin and paprika.
steps.detail: Hot pan, splash of oil. Crumble mince in and let it sit 2 min for a deep brown crust before breaking up. Add cumin, paprika, salt, pepper; stir through. Cook 6 min till no pink remains. A splash of water at the end loosens it into tacos-friendly texture.
Small dice on the tomato — drain on paper towel for 2 min if juicy so tacos don't get soggy. Halve and scoop avo, chop rough. Have a bowl of lime wedges, chopped coriander, hot sauce ready.

Small, soft corn tortillas are traditional but flour is fine. Warm each directly on a gas flame or dry pan for 15 sec per side till pliable and a few char spots. Keep warm wrapped in a clean tea towel — they go stiff fast.

Lay out components and let everyone build their own. Tortilla, mince, tomato, avo, coriander, hot sauce, big squeeze of lime. Two tortillas stacked per taco is the Mexico City trick — the second catches drippings. Eat over a plate.

title: Zucchini Slice
description: The lunchbox legend. Eggs, zucchini, bacon, a bit of cheese.
steps: Grate zucchini, squeeze out moisture.
Mix with beaten eggs, flour, diced bacon, onion, cheese.
Pour into lined tin.
Bake 35 min at 180°C until set and golden.

title: Chicken Caesar Salad
description: Crisp lettuce, smoky bacon, parmesan, a punchy dressing.
steps: Grill chicken, slice.
Fry bacon until crisp, tear.
Toast bread, cube for croutons.
Boil eggs 6 min, halve.
Toss leaves with dressing, top with all.

title: Tuna Pasta
description: Pantry-raid dinner. Spaghetti, tuna, lemon, chilli.
steps: Cook pasta.
Warm oil with garlic and chilli.
Flake in tuna, lemon zest.
Toss with pasta and parsley.

title: Red Lentil Dahl
description: The humble hero. Creamy, spiced, cheap.
steps: Soften onion, garlic, ginger.
Add spices, toast 1 min.
Tip in lentils, tomato, coconut milk, 3 cups water.
Simmer 25 min until thick.
Serve over rice.

title: Mushroom Risotto
description: Slow-stirred, silky, generous with parmesan.
steps: Soften onion and garlic in butter.
Add rice, toast 2 min.
Splash in wine, stir till absorbed.
Add stock a ladle at a time, stirring, 20 min.
Fold in fried mushrooms and parmesan.

title: Fish Tacos
description: Flaky barramundi, cabbage slaw, lime crema.
steps: Season fish with paprika, pan-fry 3 min each side.
Shred lettuce.
Stir yoghurt with lime juice for crema.
Flake fish, fill warm tortillas, top with slaw and crema.

title: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato sauce. Weekend brunch.

steps.detail: Slice onion and capsicum thinly. Cast iron or a heavy pan that can go straight to the table is ideal. Warm a glug of oil over medium, add onion and capsicum, cook 8 min till truly soft and starting to catch at the edges. Garlic (grated or minced) the last minute — burnt garlic ruins everything.

steps.content: Add paprika, then tomatoes, simmer 10 min.
steps.detail: Stir paprika into the hot veg for 30 seconds to bloom its flavour. Tip in tomatoes, crush with a spoon. Season with salt, a pinch of sugar, cracked pepper. Simmer on low for 10 min till thick enough that a spoon leaves a trail through it.

steps.content: Make wells, crack eggs into them.
steps.detail: Use the back of a spoon to make little wells in the sauce — as many as eggs you have. Crack each egg into a small bowl first (so you can fish out any shell), then slide into its well. This lets the whites sit IN the sauce, not float on top.

steps.content: Cover, cook 5 min till whites set.
steps.detail: Lid on, low heat. 5 min for whites just-set with runny yolks — the goal. Check at 4 min — they keep cooking off the heat. If your pan has no lid, a large upside-down plate or foil works.
Dot feta between eggs — it softens in the warmth. Scatter parsley if you have it. Serve straight from the pan with crusty bread for scooping. This is finger food with cutlery.

title: Pesto Pasta
description: Fresh basil, parmesan, garlic. Fifteen-minute dinner.

steps.content: Blitz basil, garlic, parmesan, oil, lemon.
steps.detail: Into a food processor (or mortar and pestle for the purists): basil leaves (ideally sunny, not refrigerator-cold), a peeled garlic clove, grated parmesan, good olive oil, a squeeze of lemon, pinch of salt. Pulse rather than blitz constantly — you want a rough paste, not a smooth sludge. Taste — salt, more lemon, more cheese until it sings.

steps.content: Cook pasta, reserve a splash of water.
steps.detail: Big pot, heavily salted water. Cook 1 min less than the packet says — the residual heat finishes it. Before you drain, scoop out half a cup of pasta water.

steps.content: Toss pasta with pesto off the heat.
steps.detail: KEY: off the heat. Hot pasta can cook the basil and dull the bright green. Drag pasta into the pesto with tongs, toss vigorously. Add splashes of reserved water as you go — the starch makes it loose and glossy rather than paste-clumped. More cheese on top, eat immediately while vivid.

title: Chicken Schnitty
description: Pub classic. Crunchy crumb, squeeze of lemon.
steps: Butterfly and bash chicken thin.
Flour, egg, crumb.
Shallow-fry in butter, 4 min each side.
Serve with lemon wedges.

title: Sweet Potato Soup
description: Velvety, warming, crowd-pleasing.
steps: Soften onion and garlic.
Add cubed sweet potato and cumin.
Pour in stock, simmer 20 min.
Blitz with coconut milk until silky.

title: Prawn Linguine
description: Garlic, chilli, white wine, parsley.
steps: Cook pasta.
Sizzle garlic and chilli in oil.
Add prawns, cook 2 min, deglaze with wine.
Toss with pasta, parsley, lemon.

title: Moroccan Lamb
description: Slow-spiced lamb with chickpeas, apricots, couscous.
steps: Brown lamb in batches.
Soften onions with spices.
Return lamb, add tomato, chickpeas, stock.
Simmer 90 min, lid off last 20.
Scatter coriander.

title: Breakfast Frittata
description: Sunday clean-out the fridge eggs.
steps: Par-boil diced potato.
Fry onion and potato until golden.
Add spinach to wilt.
Pour over beaten eggs, dot with feta.
Finish under grill 5 min till puffed.

title: Steak & Chips
description: Hot pan, butter, a pile of chips. No frills.
steps: Roast chip-cut potatoes 25 min.
Pat steaks dry, season.
Blazing hot pan, 3 min a side, butter basted.
Rest 5 min. Serve with chips.

title: Veggie Wrap
description: Lunch for humans who hate the sad desk salad.
steps: Grill haloumi slices.
Warm wrap.
Layer with avo, haloumi, cucumber, rocket, feta.
Roll tight, slice on the bias.

title: Chilli Con Carne
description: Smoky, spicy, crowd-feeding.
steps: Brown mince with spices.
Add diced onion and capsicum.
Tip in tomatoes and beans.
Simmer 40 min.
Serve with rice or corn chips.

title: Banana Bread
description: Speckled bananas, brown butter, the kindest loaf.
steps: Mash bananas.
Cream butter and sugar, add eggs.
Fold in bananas, flour, cinnamon.
Bake 55 min at 170°C.
Cool in tin 15 min.

title: Poached Eggs
description: Vinegar, simmer, spoon, done. Eggs-ellence.
steps: Simmer water with a splash of vinegar.
Swirl, slip in egg, 3 min.
Lift, rest on tea towel.
Pile on buttered toast.

title: Greek Salad
description: Tomato, cucumber, olives, feta, oregano. Nothing hiding.
steps: Chunk tomato, cucumber, red onion.
Toss with oil and lemon.
Top with feta slab.
Crack of pepper.

title: Hummus & Flatbread
description: Creamy chickpea dip. Snack-dinner energy.
steps: Blitz chickpeas, garlic, lemon, cumin.
Stream in oil till silky.
Warm wraps, tear, dip.

title: Carbonara
description: Eggs, parmesan, bacon, pepper. No cream. Fight me.

steps.content: Crisp bacon in a dry pan.
steps.detail: No oil needed — bacon renders its own fat. Lay strips in a cold pan, turn to medium. As the fat melts, the bacon shrinks and crisps. 5 min till deeply gold. Lift out with tongs. Leave the rendered fat in the pan — that's the sauce base.
Heavily salted water, rolling boil. Cook 1 min less than packet. Scoop out a full cup of pasta water before draining — carbonara needs more than most.

In a bowl, whisk 2 whole eggs with finely grated parmesan (fine, microplane if you have it — smoother sauce) and a LOT of cracked black pepper. It should look like a thick paste. No cream, never. Set aside.

steps.content: OFF heat, toss pasta with egg mix.
steps.detail: This is the critical step. The pan must be off the heat and only warm — too hot and the eggs scramble. Drag drained pasta into the pan with tongs, toss to warm through 20 sec in the bacon fat. Then pour in the egg mix, toss vigorously. Splash in pasta water as you go — the starch emulsifies with the egg into a silky sauce that clings. Scrambled eggs = too hot. Silky = right.
Tear bacon and fold through. More pepper on top, extra parmesan if you like. Eat the second it's plated — carbonara waits for no one.

title: Salmon Teriyaki
description: Sticky, glossy, rice underneath.
steps: Sear salmon skin-side down.
Mix soy, honey, grated ginger.
Add sauce, reduce until sticky.
Serve over rice with spring onion.

title: Potato Salad
description: BBQ side dish of the gods.
steps: Boil potatoes till tender.
Boil eggs 8 min, cool, chop.
Toss warm potato with yoghurt, eggs, spring onion.
Season well, scatter parsley.

title: Minestrone
description: Vegetables, beans, pasta. A ladle of summer in winter.
steps: Dice vegetables.
Soften onion, carrot.
Add tomato, beans, stock, simmer 15 min.
Add pasta, cook 8 min.
Top with parmesan.

title: Chicken Noodle Soup
description: Broth for the soul. Sick day cure.
steps: Poach chicken in stock with ginger, 20 min.
Shred.
Cook noodles in broth.
Return chicken, add carrot and spring onion.
Season well.

title: Pork Larb
description: Thai mince salad. Herby, punchy, easy.
steps: Brown mince in dry pan till crisp.
Off heat, toss with fish sauce, lime, herbs, chilli.
Spoon into lettuce cups.

title: Eggplant Parma
description: The vego pub hero. Crumbed, tomato, bubbly cheese.
steps: Slice zucchini lengthways.
Flour, egg, crumb, fry golden.
Layer in dish with tomato and mozzarella.
Bake 25 min at 200°C.
Basil to finish.

title: Breakfast Burrito
description: Eggs, beans, cheese, all rolled up.
steps: Scramble eggs.
Warm beans, mash lightly.
Pile onto warm tortillas with cheese, avo.
Roll tight, sear seam-side down.

title: Stir-Fry Beef
description: Fridge-emptying weeknight. Hot wok, fast hands.
steps: Slice beef thin, marinate in soy and ginger.
Blast in hot wok, 1 min, remove.
Stir-fry veg, add garlic.
Return beef, splash in oyster sauce.
Over rice.

title: Greek Lamb Souva
description: Skewers, tzatziki, warm bread. Backyard Friday.
steps: Cube lamb, skewer, marinate in oil and lemon.
Grate cucumber into yoghurt with garlic and mint.
Grill skewers 8 min.
Serve in warm flatbread with tzatziki.

title: Fried Egg Sandwich
description: Yolk, butter, sauce of your choosing.
steps: Butter bread, scrape one side with vegemite.
Fry eggs to your liking.
Sandwich. Mess is expected.

title: Mac & Cheese
description: Béchamel, cheddar, pasta, bubbly top. Comfort.
steps: Cook pasta.
Make béchamel: melt butter, whisk in flour, milk.
Off heat, melt in cheese.
Fold in pasta.
Top with crumbs, bake 15 min at 200°C.

title: Lentil Bolognese
description: All the depth of a slow ragù. Zero meat.
steps: Soften diced carrot, onion, garlic.
Add lentils, stock, tomatoes.
Simmer 35 min till thick.
Toss with cooked spaghetti.

title: Roast Veg Couscous
description: Meal-prep friendly, pretty, bright.
steps: Chop and roast veg 30 min, 200°C.
Make couscous per packet.
Toss veg through couscous with lemon.
Crumble feta on top.

title: Chicken Parma
description: Schnitty, napoli, ham, cheese. The classic.
steps: Crumb and shallow-fry schnitties.
Reduce tomato with garlic, salt.
Top each schnitty with napoli, ham, mozzarella.
Grill till melted.

title: Overnight Oats
description: Tomorrow-you will thank today-you.
steps: Mix oats, milk, yoghurt, honey in a jar.
Top with berries.
Lid on, fridge overnight.

title: Pancakes
description: Stack, butter, syrup. Weekend mandate.
steps: Whisk dry, then wet ingredients.
Rest 10 min.
Cook 2 min a side in buttered pan.
Stack unreasonably high.

title: Fish & Chips
description: Beer batter, crispy chips, fresh lemon.
steps: Cut chips, twice-fry or oven-bake.
Make batter with flour, beer, salt.
Dip fish, fry 4 min till crisp.
Serve with lemon.

title: Thai Beef Salad
description: Rare beef, herbs, chilli, lime. Summer dinner.
steps: Sear steak rare, rest, slice.
Toss veg and herbs with fish sauce, lime, chilli.
Fan beef over the top.

title: Butter Chicken
description: Melting tandoori-spiced chicken in a silky butter-tomato sauce.
steps: Marinate chicken in yoghurt, turmeric, curry powder, 30 min.
Sear chicken in butter, set aside.
Soften onion, garlic, ginger.
Blend tomato smooth, add with cream, simmer 10 min.
Return chicken, simmer 10 min.
Serve over basmati with coriander.

title: Lamb Kofta with Tzatziki
description: Spiced lamb skewers, cool yoghurt, warm flatbread. Friday night on the grill.
steps: Grate onion, squeeze dry.
Mix lamb, onion, garlic, spices, half the mint.
Shape onto skewers, chill 10 min.
Grate cucumber into yoghurt with remaining mint and garlic for tzatziki.
Grill skewers 4 min a side.
Wrap with tzatziki and lemon.

title: Pork Banh Mi
description: Crackly roll, spicy pork, pickled veg, coriander. Vietnamese sandwich royalty.
steps: Slice pork thin, marinate in soy, sriracha, lime.
Pickle julienned carrot and cucumber in vinegar and sugar, 15 min.
Sear pork in hot pan till caramelised.
Split baguettes, spread mayo, stack pork, pickles, coriander, chilli.

title: Rainbow Quinoa Salad
description: Fluffy quinoa, roast pumpkin, beetroot, feta, seeds. Meal-prep gold.
steps: Roast diced pumpkin and beetroot 30 min at 200°C.
Rinse and cook quinoa in 3 cups water, 15 min.
Whisk lemon juice with olive oil for dressing.
Toss quinoa, roast veg, rocket, mint with dressing.
Crumble feta, scatter almonds.

title: Sticky Date Pudding
description: The pub classic. Dark, toffee-soaked sponge. Serve with cream, obviously.
steps: Soak dates in 1 cup boiling water, 10 min.
Cream butter and sugar, beat in eggs.
Fold in flour, baking powder, date mix, vanilla.
Bake 30 min at 180°C.
Simmer brown sugar, butter, cream for sauce.
Pour over warm pudding, serve with thickened cream.

title: Pumpkin & Sage Risotto
description: Sweet roast pumpkin folded through creamy rice, crispy sage on top.
steps: Roast pumpkin 25 min at 200°C.
Fry sage in butter till crisp, remove.
Soften onion and garlic.
Toast rice, deglaze with wine.
Ladle in stock gradually, stirring, 20 min.
Fold through roast pumpkin and parmesan.
Top with crispy sage.

title: Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Kra Pao)
description: Fiery minced chicken with holy basil, over rice, crowned with a crispy egg.
steps: Mince garlic and chilli together.
Chop chicken roughly.
Fry garlic-chilli, add chicken, cook till browned.
Add sauces and sugar, toss in basil at the last second.
Fry an egg till edges crisp.
Serve over rice, egg on top.

title: Fudgy Chocolate Brownies
description: Crinkle top, molten middle. The kind you eat straight from the tin.
steps: Melt chocolate and butter gently.
Whisk in sugar, then eggs and vanilla.
Fold in flour, cocoa, chopped walnuts.
Pour into lined tin.
Bake 22 min at 180°C — middle should still wobble.
Cool in tin completely before slicing.

title: Sticky Pork Ribs
description: Low-and-slow ribs with a glossy glaze. Summer backyard mandatory.
steps: Rub ribs with paprika, sugar, salt.
Wrap in foil, bake 2 hr at 150°C.
Mix honey, soy, garlic, balsamic, sriracha for glaze.
Brush ribs with glaze, grill 10 min till sticky.
Rest 5 min, slice between bones.

title: Beetroot, Goat Cheese & Walnut Salad
description: Earthy roast beetroot, tangy ricotta, toasty walnuts. Entree energy.
steps: Roast whole beetroot in foil 40 min at 200°C.
Peel and wedge when cool enough to handle.
Toast walnuts dry 3 min.
Whisk oil, honey, balsamic, orange zest for dressing.
Toss rocket, beetroot, walnuts, dot with ricotta.

title: One-Pan Chorizo Pasta
description: Smoky chorizo, tomato, a hit of chilli. One pan, twelve minutes.
steps: Slice chorizo, render in oil till orange oil runs.
Add garlic and chilli.
Tip in tomatoes, simmer 3 min.
Add pasta and 400 ml water, cover, cook 10 min.
Uncover, toss till glossy.
Parsley and parmesan to finish.

title: Creamy Fish Pie
description: Flaked fish, peas, mash-topped. Nan's Sunday dinner distilled.
steps: Boil and mash potatoes with butter and splash of milk.
Poach fish in milk 4 min, lift out.
Make roux with flour and butter, whisk in poaching milk.
Fold fish, peas, parsley into sauce.
Pile in dish, top with mash, scatter cheddar.
Bake 20 min at 200°C till golden.

title: Eggs Benedict
description: The brunch order that announces you're serious. Poached eggs, ham, hollandaise, muffin.
steps: Separate 2 eggs; whisk yolks with lemon and dijon.
Stream in melted butter, whisking, for hollandaise.
Toast split muffins, warm ham briefly.
Poach remaining eggs in simmering vinegar water, 3 min.
Stack muffin, ham, egg, spoon hollandaise.
Scatter parsley.

title: Pumpkin Soup with Crispy Sage
description: Silky and warming. The unimpeachable winter entrance dish.
steps: Soften onion and garlic in butter.
Add cubed pumpkin, stock, simmer 25 min.
Fry sage leaves till crisp.
Blitz soup with cream till silky.
Season well, top with sage, bread on the side.

title: Ginger Beef Stir-Fry
description: Tender beef, snappy veg, the wok hissing. Dinner in ten.
steps: Slice beef thin, toss with 1 tbsp soy.
Blanch noodles.
Blast beef in hot wok 90 sec, remove.
Stir-fry garlic, ginger, broccoli, snow peas, bok choy.
Return beef with remaining soy and sesame oil.
Toss through noodles.

title: Fluffy Buttermilk Pancakes
description: Tall, cloud-soft pancakes. Weekend ritual with maple and butter.
steps: Whisk flour, baking powder, sugar.
Mix eggs, milk, melted butter, vanilla.
Combine gently — lumps are fine.
Rest 5 min.
Cook 2 min a side in buttered pan.
Stack with blueberries and maple.

title: Eggplant & Chickpea Curry
description: Silky eggplant, warming spices, creamy coconut. A vego crowd-pleaser.
steps: Dice eggplant, salt 10 min, pat dry.
Fry eggplant in oil till golden, set aside.
Soften onion, garlic, ginger; add spices.
Add tomato and coconut milk, simmer 10 min.
Return eggplant with chickpeas, simmer 10 min.
Stir in spinach; serve over basmati.

title: Seared Scallops with Pea Purée
description: Restaurant entrée at home. Caramel-crusted scallops on a vivid green purée.
steps: Blanch peas 2 min, plunge in iced water.
Blitz peas with mint, garlic, lemon, olive oil.
Pat scallops dry, season well.
Sear 90 sec a side in hot butter.
Spoon purée on plate, scallops on top, lemon wedge.

title: Zucchini Walnut Loaf
description: A sneaky-healthy cake pretending to be bread. Perfect with coffee.
steps: Grate zucchini, squeeze dry.
Whisk eggs, oil, sugar, vanilla.
Fold in flour, cinnamon, baking powder.
Stir through zucchini and chopped walnuts.
Bake 55 min at 170°C in lined loaf tin.
Cool 20 min before slicing.

title: Duck Noodle Soup
description: Rich broth, crispy-skinned duck, rice noodles. Weeknight indulgence.
steps: Score duck skin, render in cold pan till crisp, 8 min.
Flip, cook 3 min, rest.
Simmer stock with ginger and garlic, 10 min.
Cook noodles in broth, add bok choy last minute.
Season with soy.
Slice duck, fan over bowls, top with spring onion and chilli.

title: Classic Beef Burger
description: Juicy pattie, melty cheddar, lettuce, tomato, soft roll. Friday night staple.
steps: Shape mince into 4 fat patties, season.
Sear 3 min a side in hot pan.
Melt a slice of cheddar on each.
Toast rolls, spread with mayo and dijon.
Stack roll, lettuce, tomato, onion, pattie.

title: Classic Lasagna
description: Sheets of pasta, rich ragù, béchamel, a pile of parmesan. A weekend project.
steps: Sweat diced carrot, celery, onion, garlic.
Brown mince, add tomatoes, simmer 30 min.
Melt butter, whisk in flour and milk for béchamel.
Layer pasta, ragù, béchamel, cheese in a dish.
Repeat until everything is used, finishing with cheese.
Bake 45 min at 180°C, rest 15 min before cutting.

title: Provençal Ratatouille
description: Ribbons of summer vegetables baked in tomato. Looks like a painting, eats like heaven.
steps: Slice all veg into 5mm rounds.
Soften onion and garlic in olive oil, stir through tomato to make sauce.
Spread sauce in a round dish.
Fan veg slices alternating colours, in a spiral.
Cover with baking paper, bake 45 min at 190°C.
Basil and a drizzle of oil to serve.

title: Gnocchi al Pomodoro
description: Pillowy potato dumplings in the simplest tomato sauce. Twenty minutes.
steps: Warm oil with smashed garlic cloves, 2 min.
Add tomatoes, simmer 10 min.
Cook gnocchi — they float when done, 2 min.
Toss through sauce with butter.
Basil and parmesan to finish.

title: Bibimbap
description: Seoul in a bowl. Vegetables, beef, rice, a runny yolk, stir it all together.
steps: Cook rice, keep warm.
Stir-fry beef with soy and sesame oil.
Sauté julienned carrot, wilt spinach, pan-fry mushrooms separately.
Fry eggs sunny-side up.
Arrange rice in bowls, pile veg zones around, beef, kimchi, egg on top.
Gochujang on the side, stir before eating.

title: Beef Pho
description: Clear aromatic broth, thin rice noodles, pink beef cooked by the hot broth itself.
steps: Char halved onion and ginger directly on the gas, 5 min.
Simmer stock with onion, ginger, cinnamon, fish sauce, 25 min.
Strain broth, keep piping hot.
Cook noodles, divide between bowls.
Slice beef paper-thin, lay over noodles.
Pour boiling broth over; beef cooks in seconds.
Top with herbs, chilli, lime wedge.

title: Miso Ramen
description: Funky, savoury broth; springy noodles; a soft egg. Umami bomb in a bowl.
steps: Soft-boil eggs 6 min, plunge in iced water.
Sear pork belly till crisp, slice.
Simmer stock with garlic and ginger, 10 min.
Whisk miso into broth, don't boil.
Cook noodles separately, divide between bowls.
Ladle broth over noodles.
Top with pork, halved egg, wilted bok choy, nori, spring onion.

title: Chicken Katsu Curry
description: Crisp panko chicken over rice, silky curry sauce. Japanese comfort food.
steps: Flatten chicken, flour-egg-crumb.
Soften onion and carrot, add curry powder.
Stir in stock, soy, honey, simmer 10 min till thick.
Shallow-fry chicken 4 min a side till deep gold.
Slice, serve over rice, pour curry sauce over.

title: Pan-Fried Gyoza
description: Crispy bottoms, juicy pork, tangy dipping sauce. Fold a dozen, friends arrive.
steps: Salt shredded cabbage, 10 min, squeeze dry.
Mix with pork, grated ginger, garlic, soy, sesame oil, spring onion.
Place spoonful on wrapper, pleat and seal.
Fry flat-side down in oil till bottoms are gold, 2 min.
Add 1/4 cup water, cover, steam 4 min.
Uncover, crisp bases again, 2 min.
Serve with soy and vinegar.

title: Chicken Fajitas
description: Sizzling peppers and chicken, warm tortillas, let everyone build their own.
steps: Slice chicken and veg into fajita strips.
Toss chicken with spices, lime juice.
Blast in very hot pan 5 min, remove.
Char peppers and onion 4 min.
Return chicken, toss.
Serve with tortillas, sour cream, avocado, coriander.

title: Cheesy Quesadillas
description: Molten cheese, charred tortilla, lazy-dinner glory. Fifteen minutes top to bottom.
steps: Finely slice spring onion and capsicum.
Scatter cheese + veg over half a tortilla, fold.
Cook in a dry pan till gold and melted, flip, 2 min a side.
Slice into wedges, top with coriander.
Sour cream for dipping.

title: Palak Paneer
description: Vivid spinach sauce, golden cubes of paneer, warm naan. Indian date-night hero.
steps: Blanch spinach 1 min, blitz to a paste.
Cube and pan-fry paneer till gold, set aside.
Soften onion, garlic, ginger with spices.
Add tomato, simmer 10 min, then stir in spinach.
Fold in cream and paneer, warm through.
Serve over basmati.

title: Chana Masala
description: Spiced chickpeas that get better on day two. Pantry staple hero.
steps: Blitz onion, garlic, ginger to paste.
Fry paste with spices till fragrant.
Add tomato, simmer 10 min.
Tip in chickpeas + liquid, simmer 15 min.
Mash a few chickpeas to thicken.
Serve over rice with coriander.

title: Quick Chicken Biryani
description: Saffron-stained rice, marinated chicken, caramelised onion. A celebration in a pot.
steps: Marinate chicken in yoghurt + spices, 15 min.
Fry sliced onions till deep brown, set aside.
Brown chicken in the same pot.
Rinse rice, layer on top of chicken.
Pour 3 cups water over, season.
Cover tight, cook 18 min on low, rest 10.
Fluff, top with fried onion, herbs.

title: Falafel Bowl with Tahini
description: Crunchy chickpea patties, grains, pickled veg, creamy sauce. Meal-prep friendly.
steps: Blitz drained chickpeas with garlic, onion, herbs, cumin, flour.
Shape into small patties.
Pan-fry in oil till gold, 3 min a side.
Cook quinoa.
Whisk yoghurt with lemon, garlic for sauce.
Build bowls: quinoa, rocket, cucumber, falafel, sauce.

title: Chicken Shawarma Wraps
description: Spice-rubbed chicken, garlic sauce, pickled veg, all rolled tight.
steps: Mix half yoghurt with spices, lemon, garlic.
Marinate chicken 30 min or overnight.
Grill or pan-fry till charred, 4 min a side.
Slice thin.
Stir remaining yoghurt with garlic, lemon.
Warm wraps, fill with chicken, salad, sauce.

title: Classic Meat Pie
description: Puff lid, rich mince filling. A servo staple made properly.
steps: Brown mince, add onion and garlic.
Stir in flour, then stock, tomato, Worcestershire.
Simmer 20 min till thick, cool.
Line pie tin with pastry, fill with mince, top with another pastry sheet.
Egg wash lid, cut a vent.
Bake 25 min at 200°C till deep gold.

title: Beef Stroganoff
description: Tender beef, mushrooms, silky sour cream sauce, over noodles. Nan food.
steps: Slice beef thin, toss with salt and pepper.
Sear in very hot pan 90 sec, remove.
Sauté mushrooms, onion, garlic in butter.
Deglaze with stock, stir in dijon and sour cream.
Return beef, warm through.
Serve over buttered noodles with parsley.

title: Cottage Pie
description: Savoury mince under a golden mash lid. A hug in a dish.
steps: Sweat diced carrot, celery, onion, garlic.
Brown mince, add stock and Worcestershire, simmer 15 min.
Stir in peas.
Boil potatoes, mash with butter and milk.
Tip mince into dish, pile mash on top, rough with a fork.
Bake 30 min at 200°C till golden.

title: Caprese Salad
description: Mozzarella, tomato, basil, olive oil. Summer on a plate — buy the best of everything.
steps: Slice tomato and mozzarella.
Alternate on a platter with basil leaves.
Dress with oil, a few drops of balsamic, sea salt.
Eat immediately.

title: Tomato Bruschetta
description: Charred bread, sun-ripe tomato, basil. A backdrop to wine.
steps: Dice tomatoes, salt lightly, drain.
Toss with torn basil, oil, balsamic.
Grill bread both sides.
Rub hot bread with garlic clove.
Pile tomato on top.

title: Parsley Tabouleh
description: A salad that's actually herbs. Bright, green, and unreasonably good.
steps: Cover couscous in hot water, rest 10 min, fluff.
Very finely chop parsley, mint, spring onion.
Dice tomato small.
Combine everything with lemon and oil.
Season, rest 10 min before serving.

title: Cinnamon French Toast
description: Custard-soaked bread, a golden crust, maple dripping off the edges.
steps: Whisk eggs, milk, cinnamon, vanilla.
Soak bread 30 sec a side.
Fry in butter 2 min a side till gold.
Stack, dust with cinnamon, drown in syrup.
Strawberries on top.

title: Yoghurt & Granola Bowl
description: Toasted oats, honey-crunchy, cold yoghurt, bright fruit. Breakfast or honest dessert.
steps: Mix oats, chopped nuts, cinnamon.
Warm honey, oil, vanilla; toss through oats.
Spread on a tray, bake 25 min at 160°C, stirring twice.
Cool (it crisps as it cools).
Layer yoghurt, granola, blueberries in a bowl.

title: Passionfruit Pavlova
description: Crisp shell, marshmallow heart, cream, tropical fruit. The argument we'll never settle with NZ.
steps: Whip whites till soft peaks.
Add sugar a spoon at a time till glossy and thick.
Fold in vanilla.
Pile on baking paper into a round, shape edges high.
Bake 90 min at 120°C, cool in oven with door ajar.
Whip cream, pile on top.
Crown with passionfruit pulp, sliced fruit.

title: Apple & Oat Crumble
description: Soft cinnamon apples, buttery oat lid. Winter antidote.
steps: Peel and chunk apples, toss with sugar, cinnamon, lemon.
Pile in a baking dish.
Rub butter into flour, stir in oats, chopped walnuts, more sugar.
Scatter over apples.
Bake 40 min at 180°C till gold.
Cream or ice cream.

title: Tiramisu
description: Coffee-soaked sponge, mascarpone clouds, a cocoa dust. Sunday-lunch encore.
steps: Separate eggs.
Beat yolks with sugar till pale; fold in mascarpone and vanilla.
Whip whites to soft peaks, fold in.
Whip cream, fold through.
Dip biscuits in coffee, lay in dish.
Spread half the cream, repeat layers.
Chill 6 hr, dust with cocoa before serving.

title: Lemon Tart
description: Sharp, silky curd in a buttery crust. Afternoon-tea royalty.
steps: Rub cold butter into flour with 1/4 cup sugar, add yolk.
Press into tart tin, chill 30 min.
Blind-bake 15 min at 180°C.
Whisk eggs, remaining sugar, lemon juice and zest, cream.
Pour into shell.
Bake 25 min at 160°C till just set.
Chill 3 hr, slice.

title: Anzac Biscuits
description: Oat, coconut, golden syrup. Crisp-edged, chewy-hearted.
steps: Mix oats, flour, sugar in a bowl.
Melt butter with golden syrup.
Dissolve bicarb in 2 tbsp hot water, stir into butter.
Tip into dry mix, combine.
Roll spoonfuls onto trays, flatten.
Bake 15 min at 160°C till deep gold. Cool on tray.

title: Campfire Damper
description: A quick scone-loaf. Butter and honey while it's still warm.
steps: Rub butter into flour and salt.
Mix in milk till just combined.
Shape into a round on a tray, slash a cross on top.
Dust with flour.
Bake 25 min at 200°C till hollow when tapped.
Hot, with butter and honey.

title: Lamingtons
description: Sponge, chocolate icing, coconut. The most Australian afternoon tea.
steps: Cream butter and sugar, beat in eggs and vanilla.
Alternate flour and milk into batter.
Bake in a lined tray 25 min at 180°C.
Cool, cut into squares.
Whisk cocoa with 2 cups icing sugar and a splash of boiling water for glaze.
Dunk each square, then roll in coconut.
Rest on a rack till set.

title: Crispy Tofu Stir-Fry
description: Golden tofu cubes, sticky sauce, crunch. Twenty minutes, no meat missed.
steps: Press tofu 10 min, cube.
Toss in cornflour, pan-fry till crisp all over.
Stir-fry veg with garlic and ginger, 3 min.
Splash in soy, honey, sesame oil.
Fold tofu back through.
Over jasmine rice.

title: Loaded Beef Nachos
description: Melty cheese, spiced mince, sour cream, corn chips. A couch night.
steps: Brown mince with spices, add beans and tomato, simmer 10 min.
Spread chips in a baking dish.
Pile mince on top, blanket with cheese.
Bake 8 min at 200°C till bubbling.
Top with avo, sour cream, coriander, chilli, lime.

title: Buffalo Chicken Wings
description: Sticky, spicy, blue-cheese cool-down. Napkin food.
steps: Toss wings with flour, paprika, salt.
Bake on a rack 40 min at 220°C, flipping once.
Melt butter with sriracha, honey, vinegar.
Toss hot wings in sauce.
Serve with celery sticks and crumbled blue cheese in yoghurt.

title: Chicken Satay Skewers
description: Grilled chook, peanut-coconut sauce, steamed rice. Backyard favourite.
steps: Cube chicken, thread onto skewers.
Whisk peanut butter, coconut milk, soy, garlic, ginger, honey.
Brush half over skewers, marinate 10 min.
Grill 5 min a side.
Warm remaining sauce, thin with lime juice.
Serve over rice with coriander and chilli.

title: San Choy Bow
description: Pork mince in crunchy lettuce cups. A taco from the east.
steps: Finely dice mushroom and carrot.
Fry pork till browned and crisp edges.
Add garlic, ginger, veg, stir-fry 3 min.
Splash in sauces and sesame oil.
Peel lettuce cups, spoon in pork, top with spring onion.

title: Pad See Ew
description: Charred rice noodles, sweet dark soy, gai lan, beef. The wok smell of Bangkok.
steps: Soak noodles per packet.
Slice beef thin, sear hot wok 60 sec, remove.
Fry garlic, crack in eggs and scramble.
Add noodles with sauces and sugar, char 2 min.
Toss in greens and beef.
Serve with chilli vinegar.

title: Korean Beef Bowl
description: Sweet-salty bulgogi over rice, crunchy veg, a sesame finish.
steps: Whisk soy, honey, sesame oil, gochujang, garlic, ginger.
Brown beef in hot pan, pour in sauce, reduce 2 min.
Julienne carrot.
Fry eggs sunny-side up.
Bowls: rice, beef, carrot, kimchi, egg, spring onion.

title: Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: Saffron-scented chicken, lemon, olives, apricots. Slow-cooker Sunday.
steps: Brown chicken in oil, set aside.
Soften onions with spices, 5 min.
Add garlic, ginger, stock; return chicken.
Preserved-lemon-style: lemon quarters and olives on top.
Cover, simmer 75 min low and slow.
Fluff couscous, pile in bowls with tagine and coriander.

title: Chicken & Chorizo Paella
description: Saffron rice, crusty socarrat bottom, seafood optional but encouraged.
steps: Brown chicken and chorizo in a wide pan.
Soften onion, garlic, capsicum.
Stir in tomato paste, paprika, turmeric.
Add rice and stock, simmer 15 min without stirring.
Nestle prawns and peas on top, cover 5 min.
Crisp the bottom 2 min on high.
Rest, scatter lemon.

title: Tom Yum Goong
description: Hot-and-sour prawn soup. Lemongrass, lime, chilli — turns a head-cold around.
steps: Simmer stock with ginger, chillies, tomato quarters, 10 min.
Season with fish sauce.
Add mushrooms, simmer 2 min.
Add prawns, cook till pink, 2 min.
Off heat, pour in lime juice.
Finish with coriander and spring onion.

title: French Onion Soup
description: Caramelised onions, broth that tastes of time, a raft of molten gruyère toast.
steps: Thinly slice onions.
Cook in butter, low and slow, 45 min — stir often — till deeply brown.
Stir in flour, then wine; reduce.
Pour in stock and thyme, simmer 15 min.
Toast bread, pile cheese.
Ladle soup into oven-proof bowls, float toast, grill till bubbling.

title: Cream of Mushroom
description: Silky, earthy, deeply comforting. Crack black pepper liberally.
steps: Brown mushrooms hard in butter — don't crowd the pan.
Set half aside, soften onion and garlic with thyme.
Stir in flour; whisk in stock.
Return mushrooms, simmer 10 min; blitz half.
Fold in cream, reheat gently.
Top with reserved mushrooms and bread.

title: Choc Chip Cookies
description: Crisp edges, chewy centres, puddles of dark chocolate. Straight from the oven.
steps: Cream butter and sugars till pale.
Beat in eggs and vanilla.
Fold in flour, bicarb, pinch of salt.
Stir through chocolate chips.
Roll into balls on lined trays, leave space.
Bake 12 min at 180°C till edges set; cool on tray.

title: New York Cheesecake
description: Dense, tangy, 1980s-sitcom perfect. Overnight patience required.
steps: Melt butter, stir into crumbs, press into springform base.
Beat cream cheese and sugar till smooth.
Add eggs one at a time, cream, vanilla, lemon, flour.
Pour over base.
Bake 70 min at 160°C in a water bath; oven off, door ajar 30 min.
Chill overnight. Top with berries.

title: Lemon Delicious Pudding
description: Self-saucing magic: fluffy sponge above, tangy curd beneath.
steps: Cream butter, sugar, lemon zest.
Beat in yolks and flour alternately with milk.
Stir in lemon juice.
Whisk whites to soft peaks, fold in.
Pour into buttered dish, sit in water bath.
Bake 45 min at 170°C till golden and wobbly.

title: Rocky Road
description: Marshmallows, nuts, glacé cherries, a chocolate mortar. School-fête classic.
steps: Melt chocolate with butter, stir smooth.
Chop nuts, quarter any large fruit.
Stir everything through chocolate.
Tip into lined tin, press flat.
Chill 2 hr till firm.
Cut into slabs.

title: Apple Pie
description: A lattice top, cinnamon apples, cream or custard. Anyone who says otherwise is wrong.
steps: Slice apples, toss with sugar, cinnamon, flour, lemon.
Line tin with pastry, fill.
Dot with butter, lattice second pastry over top.
Egg wash, sprinkle sugar.
Bake 55 min at 190°C till deep gold and bubbling.
Rest 30 min before cutting — juices need to set.

title: Berry Smoothie Bowl
description: Cold, pink, crunchy on top. Breakfast that looks like a postcard.
steps: Blitz frozen banana, berries, yoghurt, milk, honey to thick cream.
Spoon into bowls.
Top with oats, chopped almond, fresh berries.

title: Crispy Hash Browns
description: Lacy-edge golden patties. Ideal diner breakfast, elite weekend energy.
steps: Grate potato and onion, squeeze very dry.
Season well.
Shape into patties, press flat.
Fry in hot butter and oil 4 min a side till lace-crisp.
Drain on paper, hit with sea salt.

title: The Ultimate Cheese Toastie
description: Butter-soaked sourdough, a river of cheddar, a crack of pepper.
steps: Butter outsides of bread heavily.
Spread inside with dijon.
Pile cheese between, close.
Fry over medium till deep gold, 3 min a side, pressing.
Rest 1 min, slice on the bias.

title: French Omelette
description: Pale yellow, soft folded curds, a chive whisper. Julia-approved.
steps: Beat eggs with salt and pepper.
Melt butter in a 20cm pan till foam subsides.
Pour in eggs, swirl and stir with fork, 30 sec.
When set but glossy, tilt pan, fold into a cigar shape.
Tip onto plate, scatter chives.

title: Overnight Bircher Muesli
description: Grated apple, soaked oats, yoghurt, honey. Breakfast that makes itself.
steps: Stir oats, milk, half the honey, cinnamon.
Grate in apple, add raisins, cover.
Chill overnight.
Stir through yoghurt in the morning.
Top with remaining honey and almonds.

title: Pull-Apart Garlic Bread
description: Buttery, herby, the loaf that vanishes first. Every-night energy.
steps: Slice loaf at 2cm intervals, almost all the way through.
Cream butter with crushed garlic, chopped parsley, parmesan.
Slather between every slice.
Wrap in foil, bake 12 min at 200°C.
Open foil, grill 3 min till crispy tops.

title: Yorkshire Puddings
description: Sky-high, golden, essential alongside a roast. Rest the batter.
steps: Whisk eggs, milk, flour, salt smooth. Rest 30 min.
Pour 1 tsp oil into each muffin hole.
Heat tin 10 min at 230°C till smoking.
Fill each hole 3/4 with batter.
Do not open oven. Bake 20 min.
They're done when tall and deep gold.

title: Chimichurri Steak
description: Char-kissed steak, vivid green herb sauce. South American Friday.
steps: Bring steak to room temp, season well.
Blitz parsley, oregano, garlic, chilli, vinegar, oil to a chunky sauce.
Sear steak 3 min a side in a very hot pan.
Rest 5 min, slice against the grain.
Spoon chimichurri generously.

title: BBQ Pulled Pork
description: Low-and-slow shoulder that falls into soft strands. Serve on rolls, slaw on top.
steps: Rub pork with paprika, sugar, cumin, salt.
Sear on all sides.
Whisk tomato paste, vinegar, Worcestershire, honey, stock.
Pour into pot, cover tight, bake 4 hr at 150°C.
Shred with forks, toss in pan juices.
Shred cabbage and carrot, dress with mayo and vinegar for slaw.
Stack pork in rolls, pile slaw on top.

title: Rosemary Focaccia
description: Dimpled, salty, olive-oil-drenched. The easy yeast bread.
steps: Bloom yeast in 400 ml warm water with sugar, 10 min.
Mix with flour, 1/3 cup oil, salt, no knead.
Cover, rise 2 hr.
Spread in oiled tray, dimple with fingers.
Top with oil, rosemary, sliced garlic, salt.
Rise 30 min, bake 22 min at 220°C.

title: Ham & Cheese Jaffle
description: The sealed-edge, molten-middle sandwich press legend.
steps: Butter bread outsides.
Layer ham, cheese, tomato between.
Press in a jaffle iron 5 min till outside crisp and corners sealed.
Rest a minute before biting — lava cheese incident otherwise.

title: Buttermilk Coleslaw
description: Crunchy, creamy, tangy. BBQ essential, pulled-pork friend.
steps: Shred cabbage, grate carrot, thin-slice onion.
Whisk mayo, yoghurt, vinegar, dijon, sugar.
Toss through veg, rest 15 min.
Finish with parsley and a crack of pepper.

title: Silky Mashed Potato
description: Buttery, smooth, the platform every stew deserves.
steps: Peel and chunk potatoes, cover in cold salted water.
Bring to boil, simmer 18 min till fork-tender.
Drain, dry in the pot 2 min.
Mash with warm butter and milk.
Season well, beat to silky.

title: Pork Chops with Apple & Sage
description: Pan-seared chops, caramelised apples, crispy sage. A 30-minute Sunday feel.

steps.detail: Take chops out of the fridge 15 min before cooking. Pat completely dry with paper towel — surface moisture steams instead of sears. Season both sides heavily with sea salt flakes and cracked pepper.
Quarter the apples, cut out cores, then slice each quarter into 3–4 wedges. Thick pieces hold shape and caramelise. Granny Smith or Pink Lady work best.

Heat olive oil in a heavy pan over medium-high until shimmering. Lay chops in and don't touch them for 3 full minutes — leave them alone to form a crust. Flip once. Total 6–7 min for 2cm thick. Rest on a plate tented with foil.

Same pan (the browned bits are flavour). Melt butter. When it foams, add apple wedges, whole sage leaves, and smashed garlic. Spoon foaming butter over the apples for 5 min till deep gold on both sides. The sage leaves crisp — save for garnish.

steps.content: Deglaze with dijon and honey, return chops.
steps.detail: Stir dijon and honey into the apple-butter with a splash of water. Nestle chops back in with any resting juices, warm through 1 min, spooning sauce over.

steps.content: Plate with crispy sage on top.
steps.detail: Pork, apples piled alongside, sauce spooned over, crispy sage leaves perched on top. Mashed potato or buttered greens catch the pan sauce.

title: Smoked Salmon Bagel
description: Cream cheese, ribbons of salmon, capers, red onion. Brunch royalty.

steps.detail: Slice in half horizontally. Toast cut-side-up till gold at the edges — you want structure to hold toppings without going so hard they crack. Warm, not crunchy.
Paper-thin slices are the goal. If raw onion is too punchy, drop slices into iced water for 5 min, drain, pat dry — mellows them while keeping the crunch.
At least 1 cm layer on each bagel half, right to the edges. If the cheese is fridge-cold and firm, soften at room temp 10 min or beat with a fork first.
Drape in gentle folds, not flat sheets — every bite wants pockets of fish. Three or four pieces per half.
Drained capers, a few onion slices, torn dill or parsley. Squeeze lemon, crack pepper. Don't salt — capers and salmon carry it. Eat immediately while the bagel's warm.

title: Slow-Cooked Beef Brisket
description: Falls apart under a fork. Low oven, patience, and a crown of herbs.

steps.detail: Pat brisket dry and really massage in salt, pepper, paprika. Heat a heavy oven-safe pot over high with a splash of oil until just smoking. Lay brisket in and don't touch for 3–4 min per side until each face is deep mahogany. This is where your gravy's flavour comes from. Remove to a plate.
Lower heat to medium. Add roughly chopped onion and carrot. Scrape up the browned bits (fond = flavour) as you stir. 8 min till soft; add smashed garlic last minute.

steps.content: Stir in tomato paste till brick-red.
steps.detail: Make a well, squeeze paste into the clear spot, cook 60 sec so it darkens and loses its tinny edge, then stir through. This one step lifts the whole dish.

steps.content: Add stock, Worcestershire, herbs; return brisket.
steps.detail: Pour in stock and Worcestershire. Tie herbs with string (easier to fish out) and drop in. Nestle brisket back — liquid should come halfway up, not cover. Bring to a gentle simmer.

Lid on tight, into a 150°C oven. Don't open for the first 2 hours. After 3, check: a fork should slide in with no resistance. Brisket is done when it tells you, not the clock. Up to 4 hr on a thick cut.

steps.content: Rest 30 min, slice against the grain.
steps.detail: Rest on a board tented with foil 30 min — critical. Meanwhile skim fat off the liquid, reduce on the stove till glossy. Slice across the grain (perpendicular to the fibres) into 1cm slabs. Reduced sauce spooned over.

title: Aloo Gobi
description: Potato and cauliflower roasted dry with turmeric, cumin, coriander. Nan-approved.

steps.content: Cube potato, break cauli into florets.
steps.detail: Cut potatoes into 2cm cubes — they'll cook at the same speed as the cauli florets. Break cauli into bite-sized pieces, roughly the same size as the potato for even cooking.

steps.content: Fry onion, garlic, ginger till soft.
steps.detail: Finely chop onion, grate garlic and ginger. Heat oil in a wide pan — room for everything to spread. Sauté medium 6 min till translucent and gold at the edges.
Stir in turmeric, cumin, curry powder and keep moving for 30 seconds. Spices wake up and release oils — but burn fast. Have the tomatoes ready.

steps.content: Add tomato, cook 3 min to a pulp.
steps.detail: Finely diced tomato in, stir and cook 3 min till it breaks down into a pulpy spice-base. That's the sauce coating everything.

steps.content: Toss potato and cauli to coat.
steps.detail: Tip in veg, stir till every piece is coated. Add a splash of water (1/4 cup), season, cover.
Medium-low, lid on. Veg steams in its own moisture. Stir every 5 min, splash of water if dry. After 20, potato should be tender — pierce to check.
Lid off, heat up. Let moisture cook off for 5 min — some edges will brown, that's caramelisation. Off heat, squeeze lemon, scatter torn coriander. Eat with rice or flatbread.

title: Kung Pao Chicken
description: Silky chicken, tingling chillies, crunchy peanuts, sticky glaze. Sichuan at home.

steps.detail: Cut into 2cm cubes. Toss with 1 tbsp of the soy and 1 tsp cornflour if you have it — creates a velvet coating that keeps meat juicy in high heat. Rest while you prep.
Remaining soy, rice vinegar, honey, sriracha, peanut butter. Whisk smooth, loosen with a splash of water if stiff. Sauce pours in quickly later, so ready-to-go matters.
Rinse till water runs clear. 1 part rice to 1.5 parts water, lid on, 12 min low. Off heat 5 min before fluffing. Start this first — runs in parallel.

steps.content: Blast chicken in very hot wok, 3 min.
steps.detail: Wok smoking-hot. 1 tbsp oil, swirl, chicken in a single layer. Don't stir for 90 sec — let it sear. Then toss till just cooked, 3 min total. Plate.
Lower heat. More oil if needed. Dried chillies (snap for heat, whole for flavour), garlic, ginger, white parts of spring onion. 30 sec fragrant. Diced capsicum, 2 min.

steps.content: Return chicken, sauce, toss till glossy.
steps.detail: Chicken back with resting juices. Pour sauce, toss 1 min till thick and sticky. Off heat: drizzle sesame oil, scatter green parts of spring onion.
Pile over steaming rice. Cucumber slices on the side cool it down if the dried chilli bit back.

title: Mongolian Beef
description: Crisp strips of beef in a sweet-soy glaze, spring onion everywhere.

steps.content: Slice beef thin, dust in cornflour.
steps.detail: Freeze 15 min first — easier to slice thin. Cut across the grain into 5mm strips, 7cm long. Toss in cornflour till every strip is coated — that's the restaurant-crisp exterior.
1 cm oil, hot till a test piece sizzles. Fry beef in batches — crowding drops heat and steams. 90 sec per batch till crisp-edged. Drain on paper.
Carefully pour out oil (save for other stir-fries). Wipe, keep 1 tbsp. Same pan has the fond you need.
Grated garlic and ginger over medium-high. Stir constantly for 30 seconds — you'll smell when ready.

steps.content: Add soy, sugar, splash water; reduce 2 min.
steps.detail: Pour in soy, sugar, and 1/3 cup water. Boil hard 2 min till glossy glaze that coats the back of a spoon.

steps.content: Return beef and spring onion, toss 60 sec.
steps.detail: Beef back, toss just 1 minute till glossy and coated. No longer or you'll lose the crunch. Spring onion (3cm lengths) at the very end.
Jasmine rice, beef over. Drizzle sesame oil, scatter reserved spring onion tops. Immediately while crisp.

title: Cacio e Pepe
description: Three ingredients, one silky sauce. Technique is everything.

steps.detail: LESS salt than usual — pecorino/parmesan is salty and the pasta water becomes the sauce. Cook 1 min shy of al dente. Reserve 2 CUPS of pasta water before draining — more than you think.

steps.content: Toast pepper in dry pan, 1 min.
steps.detail: Crack pepper coarsely (not fine). Dry pan, no oil. Toast 60 sec till fragrant. Off heat.
Microplane or smallest holes. Fine = smoother melt. Set aside.

steps.content: Splash pasta water into pepper pan.
steps.detail: Low heat. 1/3 cup starchy pasta water into the peppered pan. Swirl to a peppery broth.
Tongs pasta in, dripping with water (good). More splash of water. Pasta releases starch, water thickens.

steps.content: Off heat. Cheese in thirds, toss silky.
steps.detail: CRITICAL: OFF THE HEAT. Third of cheese, toss hard 20 sec to emulsify. Next third. Last. Splashes of water as you go. Sauce clings like silk. Clumps = too hot; rescue with more water and vigorous tossing.
This dish waits for no one. Plate as soon as emulsified, more cracked pepper, dusting of cheese. Eat the second it lands.

title: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
description: Garlic, chilli, olive oil. Fifteen minutes, no excuses.

steps.detail: The star. Sharp knife or mandoline, thin as a coin. Uneven chunks burn unevenly. Don't crush — gentle heat infuses, crushing = sharp allium.

steps.content: Cook spaghetti just under al dente.
steps.detail: Salt the water well (only seasoning the pasta gets). Cook 1 min less than packet. Reserve a cup of pasta water.

steps.content: Warm oil and garlic from cold.
steps.detail: Garlic AND oil in a cold pan, then turn heat to medium-low. Cold start lets garlic infuse without burning. Pale gold and fragrant, NEVER dark — dark = bitter. 3 min.
Crumble in dried chilli or a good pinch of flakes. Off heat 30 sec for infusion.

steps.content: Toss pasta with oil and water.
steps.detail: Low heat. Tongs pasta into pan with 1/4 cup pasta water. Toss hard 60 sec — starch emulsifies with oil into a loose sauce.
Off heat, torn parsley, plenty of pepper. No cheese traditionally, but a light dusting is your call. Right away.

title: Spaghetti Puttanesca
description: Salty, briny, punchy. Pantry heroes doing their best work.

steps.content: Cook pasta just under al dente.
steps.detail: Salt well. 1 min less than packet. Reserve a cup of pasta water.

steps.content: Warm oil with garlic and chilli, 2 min.
steps.detail: Thin-sliced garlic, sliced chilli, oil over medium-low till fragrant and pale gold. Don't brown — puttanesca is big flavour, no bitterness on top.
Roughly chopped olives, drained capers. Stir through the oil a minute — they release brine into the base. Whole point.
Add tomatoes (crush whole ones with your hand). Simmer 8 min till thick, raw edge gone. Taste — intensely savoury. No more salt needed.

steps.content: Pasta in with splash of water.
steps.detail: Drained pasta in with a splash of reserved water. Toss 30 sec to emulsify.
Off heat, torn parsley and plenty of cracked pepper. Traditionally no cheese — olives and capers are the salty work.

title: Salade Niçoise
description: Côte d'Azur on a plate. Tuna, eggs, green beans, olives, new potato.

steps.detail: Lower eggs gently into simmering water. Timer for EXACTLY 8 min — set yolk, still a touch custardy. Straight into iced water to stop the cook. Peel.
Halve baby potatoes. Start in COLD salted water (start in boiling and outsides cook before centres). Bring to boil, simmer 12 min till a knife slides in easily. Drain, cool.
Same pan. Beans in for exactly 3 min — snap, not softness. Straight into iced water to stay vivid green.
Jar: dijon, red wine vinegar, good olive oil, pinch of salt. Shake till creamy-emulsified, not separated. Taste on a potato; adjust salt.
Lettuce as base. Arrange potatoes, beans, halved tomatoes, olives, red onion, quartered eggs in ZONES — the French compose so each ingredient stands. Flake tuna on top in big chunks.
Spoon half the dressing over, leave the rest for the table. Scatter capers. Crusty bread alongside. Chilled rosé feels right.

title: Cobb Salad
description: Big, layered, protein-forward. Lunch that actually fills you up.

steps.detail: Season with salt and pepper. Hot pan with oil, 5 min a side to 72°C internal. Rest 5 min (juicy!). Slice across the grain.
Lay bacon flat in cold pan, medium heat. Render slowly 6 min flipping once. Drain on paper. Tear into big bits — more texture.
Simmer 8 min for just-set yolks. Iced water. Peel under a gentle stream (easier). Quarter lengthways.
Jar: olive oil, vinegar, dijon, honey, salt. Shake till emulsified. Taste on a lettuce leaf.
Chop into bite-sized pieces, spread across a wide shallow platter. Shallow = everything reachable.
Cobb tradition: each ingredient gets its own stripe across the platter. Chicken, bacon, egg, avocado, tomatoes, crumbled cheese. Scatter spring onion.
Bring dressing to table and let guests add their own — the salad looks best pre-toss. Right away.

title: Tomato Basil Soup
description: Thick, tangy, silky. Grilled cheese mandatory.

steps.detail: Halve, toss with olive oil, salt, pepper. Cut-side-up on a tray. Roast 25 min till edges char and juice pools. Deepens the flavour massively.
Butter over medium, chopped onion. Sweat 8 min till soft and translucent — no colour. Garlic final minute.
Well in the veg, squeeze paste in. Cook 90 sec in the clear spot till darker and loses tinny taste. Stir through.

steps.content: Tins + roast tomatoes + stock. Simmer 15 min.
steps.detail: Tinned tomatoes (crush with hands), roasted tomatoes with juices, stock, sugar. Simmer 15 min stirring occasionally.
Stick blender in the pot (safest) or blender in batches. Tea towel when blending hot — pressure builds. Blitz 2–3 min for silky texture.
Off heat, cream (keeps bright colour) and most of the basil (torn not chopped — bruising releases flavour). Save a few leaves. Taste, adjust salt.
Bowl it, extra cream swirl, basil. Cheese toastie on the side for dipping — non-negotiable.

title: Pork Wonton Soup
description: Feather-light dumplings, clear broth, green onion. Hands-down comfort.

steps.detail: Bowl: pork, grated ginger and garlic, 1 tbsp soy, half the sesame oil, finely chopped spring onion whites. Mix with chopsticks in one direction until slightly sticky — meat tightens up, keeps fillings juicy.

1 tsp filling centred on each wrapper. Wet two edges with water, fold to a triangle, press out air, bring the two long corners together, pinch. Little bundle. Onto a lightly floured tray so they don't stick.
Gentle simmer with a few ginger slices. Don't boil hard — graceful bubbles or wontons burst. Remaining soy, taste.
Drop in batches of 10–12. They float when done, about 3 min. Slotted spoon out.
Bok choy (split lengthways) into broth for 60 sec — vivid green, just-softened. Lift into bowls. 6–8 wontons per.
Ladle piping hot broth. Green parts of spring onion, remaining sesame oil drizzle. Chilli slices if you want heat. Steaming.

title: Vietnamese Summer Rolls
description: Rice paper wrapped around prawns, herbs, noodles. Cool, crisp, addictive.

steps.content: Cook noodles, cool in cold water.
steps.detail: Rice vermicelli only needs hot water or quick boil — check packet. Rinse cold till cool. Cut with scissors in the strainer so they're easier to wrap.
Peel cucumber and carrot, matchsticks. Halve prawns lengthways — pink curve shows through the wrapper. Pile all fillings on a large tray within easy reach.
Wide shallow bowl of warm water. Dip one for 10 sec — should still feel a touch firm lifting out. Keeps softening on the board. Too soft tears; too firm cracks.

Lay 2 prawn halves pink-side-DOWN on the wrapper (shows on top of the finished roll). Add noodles, herbs, carrot, cucumber, torn lettuce leaf. Fold bottom, then sides, then roll up tightly like a burrito.
Peanut butter, soy, lime, sriracha, warm water splash. Whisk till a ribbon-falls-off-spoon dip. Thin with more water if thick.
Summer rolls are best within 20 min of making. If waiting, cover with damp tea towel — they dry out and crack. Dip and go.

title: Carrot Cake with Cream Cheese Icing
description: Moist, spiced, piled with cream cheese frosting. Birthday-approved.

steps.content: Whisk wet: eggs, oil, sugar, vanilla.
steps.detail: Large bowl. Whisk eggs, oil, sugar, vanilla 2 min till pale — aerating gives a tender crumb.

steps.content: Sift in dry, fold in carrot, nuts, raisins.
steps.detail: Sift flour, cinnamon, baking powder, bicarb, pinch salt over the wet. Fold with spatula — DON'T beat (develops gluten, toughens). Near-combined: fold in grated carrot, walnuts, raisins.
Grease and line. Divide equally (weigh for evenness). Middle oven 40 min — skewer should come out with a few moist crumbs, not wet batter. Cool 10 min in tins, then on a rack completely.
Start with SOFT (not melted) butter. Beat smooth. Add room-temp cream cheese a quarter at a time so it doesn't split. 2 cups icing sugar and vanilla, beat 2 min fluffy. If loose, chill 15 min.
Trim domed tops flat with a serrated knife for stable stacking. Icing layer, stack second, ice top and sides. Swirl rather than smooth — more inviting. Chopped walnuts on top, classic.
Brief chill firms up icing. Hot knife dipped between slices gives neat edges. 5 days in the fridge.

title: Dark Chocolate Mousse
description: Clouds of chocolate, a whisper of cream. Eat with a small spoon.

steps.detail: Chop chocolate small for even melting. Bowl over barely-simmering water (not touching water). Stir occasionally till glossy. Off heat 5 min — hot chocolate hitting cold cream will seize.
Cold cream, cold bowl. Medium till just holding shape when beaters lift — SOFT pillows, not stiff. Over-whipping = butter. Chill.
Crack eggs one at a time. Separate whites into a spotlessly clean bowl (any fat = whites won't whip). Whisk yolks with half the sugar till pale and ribbony, 2 min.
Beat slow ramping to high. Foamy: trickle in remaining sugar. Stop at soft peaks — flops slightly when you lift the beater. Stiffer = brutal to fold.
Chocolate now cool but still fluid (finger test: warm not hot). Whisk in yolks and vanilla till glossy.

steps.content: Fold third of whites to loosen.
steps.detail: SACRIFICE FOLD — lightening the chocolate. Vigorous. Don't be precious.

steps.content: Fold rest of whites + cream gently.
steps.detail: Big sweeping strokes with a large spatula. Bottom up and across — scoop, fold, rotate bowl. Stop the moment no white streaks. Overmixing flattens.
Pretty glasses or ramekins. Chill 4 hr minimum — overnight better. Extra whipped cream and chocolate shavings on top to serve.

title: Crème Brûlée
description: The crack of caramelised sugar, silk custard below. A restaurant classic.

steps.content: Heat cream with vanilla, rest 10 min.
steps.detail: Cream into a saucepan. Pod (split, seeds scraped, both in) or extract later. Warm till first steam and edge bubbles — DON'T boil. Off heat, cover, infuse 10 min. Fish out the pod.

steps.content: Whisk yolks with sugar till pale.
steps.detail: Yolks and sugar, 2–3 min till pale and thickened. Colour change tells you sugar is dissolved. Extract goes in now if using.
VERY slowly pour warm cream into yolks while whisking constantly. Too fast = scrambled. Strain through a sieve into a jug — catches any stray cooked bits.
Divide between 4 ramekins in a deep roasting tin. Boiling water into the tin to halfway up the ramekins — bath cooks custard gently and evenly.

steps.content: Bake 40 min at 150°C till just set.
steps.detail: Middle rack. 40 min — centre should wobble like set jelly, not slosh. Carefully out, cool to room temp, then fridge 3 hr minimum (overnight perfect).

steps.content: Sugar on top, torch or grill.
steps.detail: Just before serving: 1 tbsp sugar on each, tilt to spread. Torch till deep amber (sweep, don't dwell), or very hot grill 1–2 min watching like a hawk. Caramel hardens 1 min.

steps.content: Crack with the back of a spoon.
steps.detail: The moment. Tap caramel with back of a spoon — cracks like ice. Scoop through to cold custard. Eat now — caramel softens in 15 min from condensation.

title: Strawberry Eton Mess
description: Broken meringue, cream, macerated strawberries. Effortlessly glamorous.

steps.detail: Twist out the green stem, halve (quarter if large). Toss with sugar and a squeeze of lemon. 15 min — sugar draws out juice, glossy syrup. Mash about a third with a fork for colour.
Cold cream, cold bowl. Vanilla. Medium till gentle pillows — soft, not stiff. Firms further when folded with berries.
Break meringues between your fingers into bite-sized pieces AND dust. Mix of sizes — some bits melt into cream, others crunch.
Spoon cream, berries with syrup, meringue, repeat. Layers look prettier than a fold. Don't overmix — lose the stripes.
Torn mint on top. 15 min window — meringue starts dissolving from the cream's moisture. That's fine but don't make hours ahead.

title: Margherita Pizza
description: Blistered crust, tomato, mozzarella, basil. The perfect starter recipe.

steps.detail: Yeast into 200 ml warm water (warm-to-touch, not hot). 5 min till foamy — yeast is alive. Add flour, salt, oil, mix to shaggy dough. Knead 5 min smooth, or no-knead 1 hr with a few folds. Cover, rise 1 hr till doubled.
Drain tomatoes of excess juice. Blitz or crush with olive oil, 1 grated garlic clove, salt. That's it. DON'T cook — the oven does that. Great pizza is restraint.
Stone or heaviest tray in oven, crank to max (250°C+). Preheat 20 min. A blazing surface is the difference between pub and frozen.

Divide in two. Floured bench, press out from the centre with fingertips, then backs of knuckles to stretch — edge stays a touch thicker (the crust). 25cm rounds. Rolling flattens air bubbles and toughens.
Onto baking paper. Thin sauce layer, rim bare. Tear mozzarella into chunks and dot — don't carpet. Olive oil drizzle. Fewer toppings, better pizza.
Slide onto the stone with paper underneath. 8–10 min till crust is puffed and spotted with char. Bottom should have crisp patches.
Basil on AFTER baking, never before — burns. Tear leaves (don't chop), scatter while piping hot so they wilt into the cheese. More oil if you like. Cut, inhale.

title: Quiche Lorraine
description: Buttery pastry, smoky bacon, silky custard. Picnic legend.

steps.detail: Shortcrust pressed into a 23cm tart tin (loose base = easy unmoulding). Trim, prick base, chill 10 min. Line with baking paper, fill with rice/beans as weights. Bake 12 min. Remove paper, 3 min more till dry to touch.
Sliced bacon and onion. Dry-fry bacon over medium till fat renders and edges crisp, 5 min. Add onion, 4 min soft. Drain on paper — too wet and your custard separates.
Eggs in a jug. Whisk in cream, milk, pinch of nutmeg, black pepper. Don't salt — bacon and cheese do that.
Bacon and onion first, then grated cheese. Tart tin on a baking tray to catch any leaks.
Slowly pour in, almost to the top but not spilling. Fill in the oven if the tray's already in so you don't slosh.
Middle rack. Till centre has a slight wobble, top pale gold. Overbake and custard splits. Cool 10 min before slicing — finishes setting as it rests.

title: Chicken & Mushroom Pie
description: Creamy filling, crispy puff pastry lid. Pub classic, home trophy.

steps.detail: Chicken cut into bite chunks, seasoned well. Butter in a wide pan, brown in batches — crowding steams instead of sears. 3 min per batch, one turn only. Remove.

steps.content: Sauté mushrooms hard, then leek + onion.
steps.detail: Mushrooms halved if large. Hot pan till deep gold — mushrooms need high heat or they go grey. Remove. Lower heat, more butter, sliced leek (whites and pale green), diced onion, garlic, 6 min.
Flour sprinkled over veg, stir and cook 90 sec (raw flour taste off). Slowly whisk in stock constantly — thickens as it heats. Lumps? Keep whisking, they'll smooth out.
Back in with any juices. Thyme sprigs. 10 min till chicken cooked and sauce glossy. Stir in cream and dijon. Taste for salt.
Pour into pie dish. Pastry on top, trim past the edge, tuck/crimp with a fork. Small cross in the centre to vent steam. Egg wash.
Till pastry deep gold and puffed, filling bubbling at the vent. Rest 5 min before cutting — filling is lava-hot straight out.

title: Bread & Butter Pudding
description: Buttered bread soaked in vanilla custard, sultanas, a sugar crust. Nan food at its finest.

steps.detail: Softened butter on one side of each slice — generously. Diagonal cut into triangles. Day-old bread holds its shape; fresh goes to mush.
Rub a dish with butter. Overlap bread triangles in rows, butter-side-up, raisins scattered between layers. Distributes fruit evenly.
Eggs in a jug. Whisk milk, cream, sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, pinch of salt. Strain through a sieve for extra silkiness.
Slowly pour custard over the bread, soak every slice. Press gently with a spatula. REST 15 min — bread drinks the custard, whole point. Visibly soaked through.
2 tbsp sugar evenly on top — becomes crunchy gold crust. Fresh nutmeg grated over (pre-ground fine, fresh transforms).
Middle shelf. Custard just set (slight centre wobble OK, no sloshing), peaks deep gold. 40–45 min. Rest 10 min. Warm with cream or ice cream.

title: Tandoori Chicken
description: Yoghurt-marinated, fire-charred chicken. Grill or oven.

steps.detail: 2–3 deep slashes into each piece down to the bone with a sharp knife. Marinade penetrates, heat reaches the centre faster. Pat dry first.
Yoghurt, lemon juice, grated garlic and ginger, paprika, turmeric, cumin, garam masala, big pinch of salt. Vivid orange, fragrant. Yoghurt is the trick — lactic acid tenderises.
Coat every piece — get marinade into the slashes with your fingers. Cover, fridge 4 hr min. Overnight = magic transformation. Out 30 min before cooking.
Rack on tray lets air circulate and fat drip. 30 min till marinade is dry and crusted, black-edged char patches — that char IS the flavour. 75°C internal at the thickest point.
Max top-heat grill — watch closely. Singed peaks, not burnt. 2–4 min plenty. Rest 5 min tented.
Thin red onion slices (soak 5 min in cold water to mellow), scatter with torn coriander. Extra lemon wedges. Naan, rice, or crunchy salad alongside.

title: Honey Soy Chicken Wings
description: Sticky, glossy, finger-licking wings. The weekend default.

steps.detail: The single step that transforms oven wings from rubbery to crispy. Every wing with paper towel till completely dry. Moisture is the enemy of crisp skin. Even better: salt and air-dry uncovered in the fridge for an hour first.
Soy, honey, grated garlic and ginger, sesame oil, rice vinegar, sriracha if using. RESERVE 1/3 for glazing later — never dip cooked chicken in raw marinade.
2/3 of the marinade onto wings in a bowl or bag. 30 min room temp (or overnight fridge). The sugar in honey helps browning.
Rack over a tray — no crowding, space crisps them instead of steaming. 40 min, turn once halfway. Deep gold skin, 75°C internal.

steps.content: Brush with reserved marinade, 5 min more.
steps.detail: Out, brush with reserved marinade generously. Back in 5 min till glaze caramelises to sticky lacquer. Watch — sugar burns fast.
Platter, drizzle sesame oil, sliced spring onion, sprinkle of sesame seeds. Immediately. Plenty of napkins.
